km_name: SpecificTopic Binary KM - Governance, Reporting, Due Diligence and Business Conduct
purpose: Binary Yes/No SpecificTopic decisions for the governance_reporting_conduct family. This simplified KM owns its tag-level reconciliation and emits only final Yes tags for this family.

rules: Answer every tag in this KM independently as Yes or No before producing the family final list.
Emit only tags answered Yes in final_specific_topics_from_this_family.
Do not emit candidates, centrality labels, or nearby labels as final tags.
If two tags overlap, keep both only when the source has distinct operative evidence for both; otherwise answer Yes only for the more directly satisfied decision question.
Reject incidental, background, implementation-actor-only, and keyword-only evidence even when a semantic signal appears.
The standalone SpecificTopic reconciler has been removed; this family KM is responsible for suppressing weak/adjacent labels before code consolidation.
weak_evidence_policy: Weak or not-observed tags remain in the enum, but need direct source evidence at runtime.
Do not use weak master examples as independent proof; use them only as cautionary boundary tests.
weak_or_not_observed_tags_in_this_family: Relationships with Suppliers, Including Payment Practices

tag_decisions.tag: canonical SpecificTopic value from this KM
tag_decisions.evidence_excerpt: required when answer is Yes; empty string allowed when No
tag_decisions.nearby_label_if_any: optional canonical tag that better fits the evidence

final_specific_topics_from_this_family: all tags from tag_decisions with answer=Yes, already reconciled by this family KM

tags.definition: Prevention, detection, reporting, enforcement, or governance controls for corruption, misuse of entrusted power, conflicts of interest, or improper influence.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Anti-Corruption"?
yes_when: The source centrally regulates corruption offences, anti-corruption controls, public/private corruption, conflict-of-interest rules, or corruption prevention.
There is a source-supported evidence role: regulated_object, legal_mechanism, protected_interest, direct_addressee, direct_regulated_product/facility, or final-topic ESRS mapping trace.
The tag is primary, companion, or benchmark_expected_companion; it is not merely adjacent or incidental.
no_when: The source only addresses general compliance, corporate governance, or financial crime without corruption.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.question: A source's title/scope/operative provisions centrally say: "Convention drawn up on the basis of Article K.3 (2) (c) of the Treaty on European Union on the fight against corruption involving officials of the European Communities or officials of Member States of the European Union". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Corruption"? Answer Yes/No and cite the evidence role.
test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source centrally regulates corruption offences, anti-corruption controls, public/private corruption, conflict-of-interest rules, or corruption prevention.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "anti-corruption" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source centrally regulates corruption offences, anti-corruption controls, public/private corruption, conflict-of-interest rules, or corruption prevention.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Corruption"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source only addresses general compliance, corporate governance, or financial crime without corruption.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Corruption"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

tags.definition: Prevention, prohibition, detection, reporting, or sanctions for giving, receiving, offering, or soliciting bribes.
tags.decision_question: Should this source be tagged SpecificTopic = "Anti-Bribery"?
yes_when: The source specifically regulates bribery, bribe offers, facilitation payments, gifts/hospitality as bribes, or bribery offences.

no_when: The source addresses corruption broadly without bribery-specific mechanisms, unless bribery is expressly named.
The only support is a keyword hit, source URL/domain/slug, filename, row ID, benchmark answer, generic legal boilerplate, or implementation/enforcement actor.
A narrower or different canonical label captures the operative source evidence more accurately.

test_case.reasoning_check: Yes if the quoted evidence satisfies the tag_when rule: The source specifically regulates bribery, bribe offers, facilitation payments, gifts/hospitality as bribes, or bribery offences.
forces_reasoning_about: whether the excerpt is source evidence rather than a benchmark artifact

test_case.question: A source mentions "anti-bribery" once in background, but its operative duties regulate a different subject and no duty matches "The source specifically regulates bribery, bribe offers, facilitation payments, gifts/hospitality as bribes, or bribery offences.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Bribery"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. A keyword or background reference is not enough; the label needs source-supported central evidence.

test_case.question: A source fits this exclusion: "The source addresses corruption broadly without bribery-specific mechanisms, unless bribery is expressly named.". Should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Bribery"?
test_case.reasoning_check: No. The label-specific do_not_tag_when rule excludes this case.

test_case.question: The master evidence includes a weak/indirect edge example: "the United Nations Convention Against Corruption, done at New York on 31 October 2003 [2006] ATS 2;". Standing alone, should the LLM tag SpecificTopic = "Anti-Bribery"?
test_case.reasoning_check: The master reason was classified as weak/cautionary; the LLM must look for central legal duties, not rely on weak contextual association.
